entries.summary: Prints usage and exit.
Shows version and build info.
Shows source code license and dependencies.
Shows list of models in cache.
Prints source-able bash completion script for llama.cpp.
Sets CPU threads to use during generation.
Sets threads to use during batch and prompt processing.
CPU affinity mask: arbitrarily long hex. Complements cpu-range.
Range of CPUs for affinity. Complements --cpu-mask.
Uses strict CPU placement.
Configures process/thread priority : low(-1), normal(0), medium(1), high(2), realtime(3).
Uses polling level to wait for work (0 - no polling, default: 50).
CPU affinity mask: arbitrarily long hex. Complements cpu-range-batch.
Ranges of CPUs for affinity. Complements --cpu-mask-batch.
Uses strict CPU placement.
Configures process/thread priority : 0-normal, 1-medium, 2-high, 3-realtime.
Uses polling to wait for work.
Sets the prompt context.
Sets tokens to predict.
Sets the logical maximum batch size.
Sets the physical maximum batch size.
Sets tokens to keep from the initial prompt.
Uses full-size SWA cache.
notes: (more info)

entries.summary: Configures Flash Attention use ('on', 'off', or 'auto', default: 'auto').
Toggles whether to enable internal libllama performance timings.
Toggles whether to process escapes sequences (\n, \r, \t, \', \", \\).
RoPE frequency scaling method, defaults to linear unless specified by the model.
RoPE context scaling factor, expands context by a factor of N.
RoPE base frequency, used by NTK-aware scaling.
RoPE frequency scaling factor, expands context by a factor of 1/N.
YaRN: original context size of model.
YaRN: extrapolation mix factor.
YaRN: scale sqrt(t) or attention magnitude.
YaRN: high correction dim or alpha.
YaRN: low correction dim or beta.
Toggles whether to enable KV cache offloading.
Toggles whether to enable weight repacking.
Bypass host buffer allowing extra buffers to be used.
KV cache data type for K.
KV cache data type for V.
KV cache defragmentation threshold (DEPRECATED).
Forces system to keep model in RAM rather than swapping or compressing.
Toggles whether to memory-map model. (if mmap disabled, slower load but may reduce pageouts if not using mlock).
Uses DirectIO if available.
Applies optimizations that optimizations that help on some NUMA systems.
notes: - distribute: spread execution evenly over all nodes
- isolate: only spawn threads on CPUs on the node that execution started on
- numactl: use the CPU map provided by numactl
if run without this previously, it is recommended to drop the system page cache before using this
see https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/issues/1437

entries.summary: Comma-separated list of devices to use for offloading (none = don't offload).
notes: use --list-devices to see a list of available devices

entries.summary: Prints list of available devices and exit.
Override tensor buffer type.
Keep all Mixture of Experts (MoE) weights in the CPU.
Keep the Mixture of Experts (MoE) weights of the first N layers in the CPU.
Caps layers to store in VRAM, either an exact number, 'auto', or 'all'.
How to split the model across multiple GPUs, one of:.
notes: - none: use one GPU only
- layer (default): split layers and KV across GPUs
- row: split rows across GPUs

entries.summary: Fraction of the model to offload to each GPU, comma-separated list of proportions, e.g. 3,1.
The GPU to use for the model (with split-mode = none), or for intermediate results and KV (with split-mode = row).
Toggles whether to adjust unset arguments to fit in device memory ('on' or 'off', default: 'on').
Target margin per device for --fit, comma-separated list of values, single value is broadcast across all devices, default: 1024.
Minimum ctx size that can be set by --fit option, default: 4096.
Check model tensor data for invalid values.
Advanced option to override model metadata by key. to specify multiple overrides, either use comma-separated values.
notes: types: int, float, bool, str. example: --override-kv tokenizer.ggml.add_bos_token=bool:false,tokenizer.ggml.add_eos_token=bool:false

entries.summary: Toggles whether to offload host tensor operations to device.
Specifies file path for LoRA adapter (use comma-separated values to load multiple adapters).
Specifies file path for LoRA adapter with user defined scaling (format: FNAME:SCALE,...).
notes: note: use comma-separated values

entries.summary: Adds a control vector.
notes: note: use comma-separated values to add multiple control vectors

entries.summary: Adds a control vector with user defined scaling SCALE.
notes: note: use comma-separated values (format: FNAME:SCALE,...)

entries.summary: Layer range to apply the control vector(s) to, start and end inclusive.
Specifies local model path.
Specifies remote model download URL.
Docker Hub model repository. repo is optional, default to ai/. quant is optional, default to :latest.
notes: example: gemma3

entries.summary: Hugging Face model repository; quant is optional, case-insensitive, default to Q4_K_M, or falls back to the first file in the repo if Q4_K_M doesn't exist.
notes: mmproj is also downloaded automatically if available. to disable, add --no-mmproj
example: ggml-org/GLM-4.7-Flash-GGUF:Q4_K_M

entries.summary: Same as --hf-repo, but for the draft model.
Hugging Face model file. If specified, it will override the quant in --hf-repo.
Hugging Face model repository for the vocoder model.
Hugging Face model file for the vocoder model.
Hugging Face access token.
Log disable.
Log to file.
Configures colored logging ('on', 'off', or 'auto', default: 'auto').
notes: 'auto' enables colors when output is to a terminal

entries.summary: Configures verbosity level to infinity (i.e. log all messages, useful for debugging).
Runs in offline mode: forces use of cache, prevents network access.
Configures the verbosity threshold. Messages with a higher verbosity will be ignored. Values:.
notes: - 0: generic output
- 1: error
- 2: warning
- 3: info
- 4: debug

entries.summary: Enable prefix in log messages.
Enable timestamps in log messages.
KV cache data type for K for the draft model.
KV cache data type for V for the draft model.
Samplers that will be used for generation in the order, separated by ';'.
RNG seed.
Simplified sequence for samplers that will be used.
Ignore end of stream token and continue generating (implies --logit-bias EOS-inf).
Temperature.
Top-k sampling.
Top-p sampling.
Min-p sampling.
Top-n-sigma sampling.
Xtc probability.
Xtc threshold.
Locally typical sampling, parameter p.
Last n tokens to consider for penalize.
Penalize repeat sequence of tokens.
Repeat alpha presence penalty.
Repeat alpha frequency penalty.
Configures DRY sampling multiplier.
Configures DRY sampling base value.
Configures allowed length for DRY sampling.
Configures DRY penalty for the last n tokens.
Adds sequence breaker for DRY sampling, clearing out default breakers ('\n', ':', '"', '*') in the process; use "none" to not use any sequence breakers.
Adaptive-p: select tokens near this probability (valid range 0.0 to 1.0; negative = disabled).
notes: (more info)

entries.summary: Adaptive-p: decay rate for target adaptation over time. lower values are more reactive, higher values are more stable.
notes: (valid range 0.0 to 0.99)

entries.summary: Dynamic temperature range.
Dynamic temperature exponent.
Uses Mirostat sampling.
notes: Top K, Nucleus and Locally Typical samplers are ignored if used.

entries.summary: Mirostat learning rate, parameter eta.
Mirostat target entropy, parameter tau.
Modifies the likelihood of token appearing in the completion.
notes: i.e. --logit-bias 15043+1 to increase likelihood of token ' Hello'
or --logit-bias 15043-1 to decrease likelihood of token ' Hello'

entries.summary: BNF-like grammar to constrain generations (see samples in grammars/ dir).
File to read grammar from.
JSON schema to constrain generations (https://json-schema.org/), e.g. {} for any JSON object.
notes: For schemas w/ external $refs, use --grammar + example/json_schema_to_grammar.py instead

entries.summary: File containing a JSON schema to constrain generations (https://json-schema.org/), e.g. {} for any JSON object.
notes: For schemas w/ external $refs, use --grammar + example/json_schema_to_grammar.py instead

entries.summary: Enable backend sampling (experimental).
Specifies file path for static lookup cache to use for lookup decoding (not updated by generation).
Specifies file path for dynamic lookup cache to use for lookup decoding (updated by generation).
Max number of context checkpoints to create per slot.
Create a checkpoint every n tokens during prefill (processing), -1 to disable.
Configures the maximum cache size in MiB.
Uses single unified KV buffer shared across all sequences.
entries.default: enabled if number of slots is auto

entries.summary: Save and clear idle slots on new task.
entries.default: enabled, requires unified KV and cache-ram

entries.summary: Toggles whether to use context shift on infinite text generation.
Halt generation at PROMPT, return control in interactive mode.
Special tokens output enabled.
Toggles whether to perform warmup with an empty run.
Uses Suffix/Prefix/Middle pattern for infill (instead of Prefix/Suffix/Middle) as some models prefer this.
Pooling type for embeddings, use model default if unspecified.
Sets server slots.
Toggles whether to enable continuous batching (a.k.a dynamic batching).
Specifies file path for a multimodal projector file. see tools/mtmd/README.md.
notes: note: if -hf is used, this argument can be omitted

entries.summary: Specifies URL for a multimodal projector file. see tools/mtmd/README.md.
Toggles whether to use multimodal projector file (if available), useful when using -hf.
Toggles whether to enable GPU offloading for multimodal projector.
Sets minimum tokens each image can take, only used by vision models with dynamic resolution.
Caps tokens each image can take, only used by vision models with dynamic resolution.
Override tensor buffer type for draft model.
Keep all Mixture of Experts (MoE) weights in the CPU for the draft model.
Keep the Mixture of Experts (MoE) weights of the first N layers in the CPU for the draft model.
Configures model name aliases, comma-separated (to be used by API).
Configures model tags, comma-separated (informational, not used for routing).
Ip address to listen, or bind to an UNIX socket if the address ends with .sock.
Port to listen.
Allow multiple sockets to bind to the same port.
Specifies file path for serve static files from (default: ).
Prefix path the server serves from, without the trailing slash (default: ).
JSON that provides default WebUI settings (overrides WebUI defaults).
JSON file that provides default WebUI settings (overrides WebUI defaults).
Experimental: whether to enable MCP CORS proxy - do not enable in untrusted environments.
Experimental: whether to enable built-in tools for AI agents - do not enable in untrusted environments.
notes: specify "all" to enable all tools
available tools: read_file, file_glob_search, grep_search, exec_shell_command, write_file, edit_file, apply_diff

entries.summary: Toggles whether to enable the Web UI.
Restrict to only support embedding use case; use only with dedicated embedding models.
Enable reranking endpoint on server.
API key to use for authentication, multiple keys can be provided as a comma-separated list.
Specifies file path for file containing API keys.
Specifies file path for file a PEM-encoded SSL private key.
Specifies file path for file a PEM-encoded SSL certificate.
Sets additional params for the json template parser, must be a valid json object string, e.g. '{"key1":"value1","key2":"value2"}'.
Server read/write timeout in seconds.
Sets threads used to process HTTP requests.
Toggles whether to enable prompt caching.
Min chunk size to attempt reusing from the cache via KV shifting, requires prompt caching to be enabled.
notes: (card)

entries.summary: Enable prometheus compatible metrics endpoint.
Enable changing global properties via POST /props.
Expose slots monitoring endpoint.
Specifies file path for save slot kv cache.
Directory for loading local media files; files can be accessed via file:// URLs using relative paths.
Directory containing models for the router server.
Specifies file path for INI file containing model presets for the router server.
For router server, maximum number of models to load simultaneously.
For router server, whether to automatically load models.
Toggles whether to use jinja template engine for chat.
Controls whether thought tags are allowed and/or extracted from the response, and in which format they're returned; one of:.
notes: - none: leaves thoughts unparsed in message.content
- deepseek: puts thoughts in message.reasoning_content
- deepseek-legacy: keeps <think> tags in message.content while also populating message.reasoning_content

entries.summary: Uses reasoning/thinking in the chat ('on', 'off', or 'auto', default: 'auto' (detect from template)).
Token budget for thinking: -1 for unrestricted, 0 for immediate end, N>0 for token budget.
Message injected before the end-of-thinking tag when reasoning budget is exhausted.
Configures custom jinja chat template.
notes: if suffix/prefix are specified, template will be disabled
only commonly used templates are accepted (unless --jinja is set before this flag):
list of built-in templates:

bailing, bailing-think, bailing2, chatglm3, chatglm4, chatml, command-r, deepseek, deepseek-ocr, deepseek2, deepseek3, exaone-moe, exaone3, exaone4, falcon3, gemma, gigachat, glmedge, gpt-oss, granite, grok-2, hunyuan-dense, hunyuan-moe, kimi-k2, llama2, llama2-sys, llama2-sys-bos, llama2-sys-strip, llama3, llama4, megrez, minicpm, mistral-v1, mistral-v3, mistral-v3-tekken, mistral-v7, mistral-v7-tekken, monarch, openchat, orion, pangu-embedded, phi3, phi4, rwkv-world, seed_oss, smolvlm, solar-open, vicuna, vicuna-orca, yandex, zephyr

entries.summary: Configures custom jinja chat template file.
notes: if suffix/prefix are specified, template will be disabled
only commonly used templates are accepted (unless --jinja is set before this flag):
list of built-in templates:

bailing, bailing-think, bailing2, chatglm3, chatglm4, chatml, command-r, deepseek, deepseek-ocr, deepseek2, deepseek3, exaone-moe, exaone3, exaone4, falcon3, gemma, gigachat, glmedge, gpt-oss, granite, grok-2, hunyuan-dense, hunyuan-moe, kimi-k2, llama2, llama2-sys, llama2-sys-bos, llama2-sys-strip, llama3, llama4, megrez, minicpm, mistral-v1, mistral-v3, mistral-v3-tekken, mistral-v7, mistral-v7-tekken, monarch, openchat, orion, pangu-embedded, phi3, phi4, rwkv-world, seed_oss, smolvlm, solar-open, vicuna, vicuna-orca, yandex, zephyr

entries.summary: Forces a pure content parser, even if a Jinja template is specified; model will output everything in the content section, including any reasoning and/or tool calls.
Toggles whether to prefill the assistant's response if the last message is an assistant message.
notes: when this flag is set, if the last message is an assistant message then it will be treated as a full message and not prefilled

entries.summary: How much the prompt of a request must match the prompt of a slot in order to use that slot.
Load LoRA adapters without applying them (apply later via POST /lora-adapters).
Sets seconds of idleness after which the server will sleep.
Sets threads to use during generation.
Sets threads to use during batch and prompt processing.
Sets tokens to draft for speculative decoding.
Sets minimum draft tokens to use for speculative decoding.
Minimum speculative decoding probability (greedy).
Sets the prompt context for the draft model.
Comma-separated list of devices to use for offloading the draft model (none = don't offload).
notes: use --list-devices to see a list of available devices

entries.summary: Caps draft model layers to store in VRAM, either an exact number, 'auto', or 'all'.
Draft model for speculative decoding.
Translate the string in TARGET into DRAFT if the draft model and main model are not compatible.
Type of speculative decoding to use when no draft model is provided.
Ngram size N for ngram-simple/ngram-map speculative decoding, length of lookup n-gram.
Ngram size M for ngram-simple/ngram-map speculative decoding, length of draft m-gram.
Minimum hits for ngram-map speculative decoding.
Vocoder model for audio generation.
Uses guide tokens to improve TTS word recall.
Uses default EmbeddingGemma model (note: can download weights from the internet).
Uses default Qwen 2.5 Coder 1.5B (note: can download weights from the internet).
Uses default Qwen 2.5 Coder 3B (note: can download weights from the internet).
Uses default Qwen 2.5 Coder 7B (note: can download weights from the internet).
Uses Qwen 2.5 Coder 7B + 0.5B draft for speculative decoding (note: can download weights from the internet).
Uses Qwen 2.5 Coder 14B + 0.5B draft for speculative decoding (note: can download weights from the internet).
Uses default Qwen 3 Coder 30B A3B Instruct (note: can download weights from the internet).
Uses gpt-oss-20b (note: can download weights from the internet).
Uses gpt-oss-120b (note: can download weights from the internet).
Uses Gemma 3 4B QAT (note: can download weights from the internet).
Uses Gemma 3 12B QAT (note: can download weights from the internet).
